draft.introduction: A scribe asks Jesus which commandment is greatest. Jesus answers with the Shema and the love commandment, and the scribe’s agreement earns rare praise: “You are not far from the kingdom of God.”

translation.text: And one of the scribes came up and asked him, “Which commandment is the most important of all?” Jesus answered, “The most important is: Hear, O Israel, the Lord our God, the Lord is one. And you shall love the Lord your God with all your heart and with all your soul and with all your mind and with all your strength. The second is this: You shall love your neighbor as yourself.” And when Jesus saw that he answered wisely, he said to him, “You are not far from the kingdom of God.”

translator_notes.note: Mark’s Jesus begins with the Shema—the only Gospel version to include this preamble. The affirmation of monotheism before the love command is theologically significant: love of God presupposes the oneness of God.
